codeInvestissements.title: Investment Code
codeInvestissements.subtitle: Summary of eligible activities, infrastructures, guarantees and tax benefits (ref. Law 2004-06, CGI art. 250, 252, 263, 373).
codeInvestissements.section1Intro: Click on a threshold or category to view details.
catPrimaireItems: Storage, packaging and processing of local products (plant, animal, fishery)
catServicesItems: Installation and maintenance of industrial equipment
catMinerauxItems: Extraction or transformation of mineral substances
codeInvestissements.section2Intro: Definition of eligible projects by infrastructure type.
infraCommerciauxItems: Delivery areas, access roads and supply routes
infraAeroportuairesItems: Technical and commercial premises for airport activity
garantiesItems: Protection against nationalisation, expropriation or requisition
Guarantee of transfer of capital and remuneration
Rights and freedoms of the enterprise (economic and competitive freedom)
Obligations: public order, environmental and consumer protection, standards, provision of information for control
codeInvestissements.creationDef: Newly created entity in the implementation phase of an eligible investment programme.
codeInvestissements.extensionDef: Approved programme by an existing company: at least 25% increase in production capacity or value of fixed assets, or investment in equipment of at least 100 M FCFA.
avantageCreationRealisation: Customs exemptions on production materials and equipment (excl. consumables) — 3 years
VAT suspension — 3 years; exemption for agricultural sector (production and irrigation)
avantageCreationExploitation: CFCE exemption: 5 years (8 years if at least 200 jobs created or 90% of jobs outside Dakar region)
Exempt utility vehicles (1 at start + 1 per 150 M FCFA invested)
Tax credit: 40% deduction of investments over 5 years, capped at 50% of taxable profit (70% outside Dakar)
avantageExtensionRealisation: Customs exemptions on production materials and equipment — 3 years
VAT suspension — 3 years; exemption for agricultural projects
avantageExtensionExploitation: CFCE exemption: 5 years (8 years if at least 100 jobs created or 90% outside Dakar)
Tax credit: 30% deduction of investments over 5 years, capped at 50% of taxable profit (70% outside Dakar)

startupAct.title: Startup Act
startupAct.subtitle: Law No. 2020-01 of 6 January 2020 on the creation and promotion of startups in Senegal
startupAct.ch2Title: Chapter II – Institutional and organisational framework
startupAct.art1: This Law sets out the modalities for the creation and promotion of startups in Senegal based on creativity, innovation, the use of new technologies, the achievement of high added value and competitiveness at national and international level.
startupAct.art2: This Law applies to any startup created on Senegalese territory whose capital is at least one-third (1/3) held by Senegalese nationals or residents, or by legal entities with their registered office in Senegal. It also applies to any startup created by Senegalese nationals abroad whose capital is at least 50% held by them.
startupAct.art3Def: For the purposes of this Law:
startupAct.art3Promoteur: startup promoter: individual carrying an innovative project with high potential, implemented through a legally constituted Senegalese company;
startupAct.art3Startup: startup: innovative and agile company, legally constituted for less than eight years, with high growth potential, seeking a disruptive business model and adapted financing mechanisms;
startupAct.art3Enregistree: registered startup: startup registered with an approved public or private support structure;
startupAct.art3Labellee: labelled startup: startup to which the competent body has issued the label.
startupAct.art4: An evaluation, support and coordination Commission is established, inclusive of all public, private and societal stakeholders and the expertise needed for startup development. Its organisation and operation are set by decree.
startupAct.art5: The Commission sets up a dedicated platform, accessible online, for registration and labelling. The platform facilitates access to information and manages a startup directory.
startupAct.art6: Public or private support structures are governed by decree and may benefit from incentive measures. They are approved by the Commission.
startupAct.art7: Any company meeting the criteria of Article 2 may register with approved support structures. Any legally registered startup may apply for labelling from the Commission. Technical standards for the label are defined by the Commission and approved by decree.
startupAct.art8Intro: Any registered or labelled startup benefits from incentive measures. These include:
art8Items: customs and social benefits (Labour Code, Customs Code)
facilitated access to public procurement (Public Procurement Code)
startupAct.art9: Companies wishing to benefit must comply with this Law. They are subject to simplified registration and labelling procedures established by the Commission.
art10Items: cover any support needed during the startup phase
startupAct.art10Social: Mechanisms for covering social security contributions and charges are established for registered startups.
startupAct.art10Fiscal: Registered startups benefit from tax advantages and favourable customs regimes under the CGI and applicable laws.
startupAct.art11: The Commission sets up a training and capacity-building platform for registered startups: database, list of experts, trainers and mentors on finance, marketing, communication, business plan development.
startupAct.art12Intro: Public and private funds are established for labelled startups to finance eligible startups and guarantee:
art12Items: loans, financing and equity participations from investment companies (ceiling set by decree)
startupAct.art12Douane: Labelled startups may benefit from customs advantages during the validity of the label.
startupAct.art13Intro: Labelled startups benefit from preferential access to public procurement.
startupAct.art13Marge: A 5% preference margin is granted to labelled startups participating in tenders (public procurement, public service delegation, partnership contracts). This percentage is cumulative with other advantages (maximum 25%).
startupAct.art13SousTraitance: A bidder subcontracting 30% of services to one or more labelled startups, or bidding in consortium with a startup, may benefit from a 5% preference margin, cumulative with regulations. Not applicable when in consortium with a non-startup company.
startupAct.art14: The Commission, with stakeholders and credit institutions, defines and implements alternative financing strategies and mechanisms for startups.
startupAct.art15: Any registered or labelled startup benefiting from incentives must comply with obligations set by decree. Any breach is sanctioned proportionally.
startupAct.art16: The label is withdrawn from startups no longer meeting eligibility criteria. Withdrawal entails loss of all benefits. Withdrawal procedures are set out in technical standards approved by decree.
startupAct.art17: A startup in breach may request and obtain regularisation from the Commission. Conditions are set by decree.
startupAct.art18: This Law applies to any company already constituted on national territory and eligible for startup status.
startupAct.art19: The implementing provisions of this Law are set by decree.

quorum.title: SA quorums & majorities
quorum.hint: Select the type of meeting and the convocation, then enter the total number of shares, the shares present and the votes in favour.
quorum.specialDecision: Decision on capital reduction or articles of association (constitutive GM) – unanimity required.
quorum.votesFor: Votes in favour of the resolution
quorum.noQuorumRule: No quorum rule: the meeting may deliberate regardless of the number of shares present.
quorum.quorumRule: Required quorum: at least {{percent}} % of shares
quorum.quorumOk: Quorum met: the meeting can validly deliberate.
quorum.quorumFail: Quorum not met: the meeting cannot deliberate.
quorum.majorityUnanimity: Required majority: unanimity of votes present.
quorum.majorityTwoThirds: Required majority: at least 2/3 of votes present.
quorum.majoritySimple: Required majority: more than half of votes present.
quorum.majorityOk: Majority met: the resolution can be adopted.
quorum.majorityFail: Majority not met: the resolution is rejected.
quorum.summaryNoQuorum: Quorum not met: a new meeting must be convened.
quorum.summaryAllOk: Quorum and majority met: the resolution can be adopted.
quorum.summaryNoMajority: Quorum met but majority not met: the resolution is rejected.

ratios.title: Usual ratios - Financial diagnostic & profitability
ratios.selectRatio: Please select the ratio to calculate.

descriptions.valueAdded: This ratio measures the company's contribution to the value of production.
descriptions.tradingMargin: This ratio measures the margin on sales relative to revenue.
descriptions.ebitda: This ratio measures operating profitability before financial charges and depreciation.
descriptions.netResult: This ratio measures the share of revenue remaining as net result.

ratios.waiting: Enter amounts to display the ratio and chart.

cabinet.title: The Firm
cabinet.intro1: ADOC Audit & Conseil is a structured and independent firm, operating in Senegal and across the West African sub-region. The firm supports public and private organizations facing complex governance, compliance, performance and transformation challenges.
cabinet.intro2: ADOC's positioning is based on a central conviction: the quality of decisions depends directly on the reliability of information, the robustness of analysis and the independence of advice.
cabinet.tip: Tip: hover over the carousel to pause. Click on an image to enlarge it.
cabinet.approach2: In-depth understanding of organizational realities in West Africa.
cabinet.approach3: Commitment to quality and confidentiality on sensitive assignments.

salarySimulator.title: Senegal Pay Simulator
salarySimulator.disclaimer: Simulator compliant with Law No. 2022-19 of 27 May 2022 (Senegal)

indemniteLicenciement.title: Severance pay calculator
indemniteLicenciement.info: Average salary = gross social ÷ 12. Tenure = (termination date - hire date) in days ÷ 365. Rates: 0-5 yrs 25%, 5-10 yrs 30%, 10+ yrs 40%.
indemniteLicenciement.fillForm: Enter gross social salary, hire date and termination date to calculate.

indemniteFinCDD.title: Fixed-term contract end indemnity
indemniteFinCDD.info: The indemnity equals 7% of the total gross remuneration due to the worker during the contract (Labour Code).
indemniteFinCDD.nonDue1: In the cases covered by paragraphs 1, 2, 3 and 5 of Art. L.42 and Art. L.43;
indemniteFinCDD.nonDue2: When the worker refuses to accept a permanent contract for the same or similar position with at least equal pay;
indemniteFinCDD.nonDue3: In case of early termination due to the worker's initiative or serious misconduct.
indemniteFinCDD.fillForm: Enter the total gross remuneration due to calculate.
